sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: 스타벅스는 'Back to Starbucks' 전략 하에 중국 사업 합작법인 전환 및 효율화 중심의 구조조정을 지속하고 있습니다.

keyPoints.text: 브랜드 경험 강화와 효율성 제고를 목표로 하는 'Back to Starbucks' 전략을 통해 매장 포트폴리오를 재평가하고 성과가 부진한 매장을 폐쇄하는 구조조정을 진행 중입니다.
중국 시장 내 소매 운영 효율화를 위해 Boyu Capital과 합작법인을 설립하기로 합의했으며, 스타벅스는 지분 40%를 보유하고 브랜드 및 지식재산권을 유지할 예정입니다.
경영진 및 주요 임원들은 Rule 10b5-1 거래 계획에 따라 주식 매도 계획을 수립하였으며, 이는 사전 공시된 규정에 따라 운영됩니다.

riskChanges.text: 기존 10-K에 공시된 위험 요인 외에 이번 분기 중 중대한 위험 변화는 발생하지 않았습니다.
임원진의 주식 거래 계획은 사전 정의된 규칙에 따라 이루어지며, 사망, 파산, 또는 거래 계획 해지 통보 시 자동 종료되는 리스크 관리 조항을 포함합니다.

businessChanges.text: 중국 사업 부문을 매각 예정 자산(held for sale)으로 분류함에 따라 관련 자산의 감가상각 및 사용권 자산 상각을 중단하였습니다.
매장 포트폴리오 최적화를 위해 브랜드 일관성과 재무적 성과 기준을 충족하지 못하는 매장을 선별적으로 폐쇄하는 작업을 수행하고 있습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 당분기 매출은 99억 1,510만 달러, 영업이익은 8억 9,080만 달러를 기록하였습니다.
순이익은 2억 9,330만 달러이며, 총자산 규모는 322억 2,830만 달러 수준입니다.

watchItems.text: 중국 합작법인 전환이 향후 스타벅스의 글로벌 수익성 및 자산 효율성에 미칠 영향과 'Back to Starbucks' 전략의 매장 폐쇄 비용이 영업이익에 미치는 변동성을 주시해야 합니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: Quarter ended 2025-12-28 revenue = 99억 1,510만 달러 (USD 9.915B; 9,915 million), XBRL range 2025-09-29..2025-12-28.
Quarter ended 2025-12-28 operating income = 8억 9,080만 달러 (USD 0.891B; 891 million), XBRL range 2025-09-29..2025-12-28.
Quarter ended 2025-12-28 net income = 2억 9,330만 달러 (USD 0.293B; 293 million), XBRL range 2025-09-29..2025-12-28.
Quarter ended 2025-12-28 total assets = 322억 2,830만 달러 (USD 32.228B; 32,228 million), XBRL range instant..2025-12-28.

Item 1A. Risk Factors in our 10-K. There have been no material changes to the risk factors disclosed in our 10-K. Item 2. Unregistered Sales of Equity Securities and Use of Proceeds Shares under our ongoing share repurchase program may be repurchased in open market transactions, including pursuant to a trading plan adopted in accordance with Rule 10b5-1 of the Exchange Act, or through privately negotiated transactions. The timing, manner, price, and amount of repurchases will be determined at our discretion and the share repurchase program may be suspended, terminated, or modified at any time for any reason. During the first fiscal quarter ended December 28, 2025, there was no share repurchase activity. Item 3. Defaults upon Senior Securities None. Item 4. Mine Safety Disclosures Not applicable. Item 5. Other Information Insider Adoption or Termination of Trading Arrangements: During the

fiscal quarter ended December 28, 2025, none of our directors or officers informed us of the adoption or termination of a “Rule 10b5-1 trading arrangement” or “non-Rule 10b5-1 trading arrangement,” as those terms are defined in Regulation S-K, Item 408, except as described in the table below: Name & Title Date Adopted Character of Trading Arrangement (1) Aggregate Number of Shares of Common Stock to be Purchased or Sold Pursuant to Trading Arrangement Duration Other Material Terms Date Terminated Brady Brewer, chief executive officer, Starbucks International December 11, 2024 Rule 10b5-1 Trading Arrangement Previously Disclosed Previously Disclosed Previously Disclosed December 3, 2025 December 3, 2025 Rule 10b5-1 Trading Arrangement Up to 22,290 shares to be sold (2) December 31, 2026 (4)(5) N/A N/A Sara Kelly, executive vice president, chief partner officer December 3, 2025 Rule 10b5-

management’s discussion and analysis of the consolidated financial statements in the 10-K. The results of operations for the quarter ended December 28, 2025 are not necessarily indicative of the results of operations that may be achieved for the entire fiscal year ending September 27, 2026 (“fiscal 2026”). Restructuring In the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024 , we announced our “Back to Starbucks” strategy, which was implemented with the goal to bring customers back to our stores and return to growth by revitalizing coffeehouses, enhancing the customer experience, and improving efficiency. As part of this strategy, during the second quarter of fiscal 2025 , we announced our plan to restructure our support organization in an effort to operate more efficiently, increase accountability, reduce complexity, and drive better integration, which resulted in a reduction in our support partner workfo

rce. In the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025 , we announced a restructuring plan involving the closure of coffeehouses, and the further transformation of our support organization, as part of the Company’s “Back to Starbucks” strategy. We assessed our existing store portfolio with respect to both whether coffeehouses had a viable path to offering the physical environment consistent with the brand and a clear path to financial performance, and we closed, or plan to close, coffeehouses that did not meet these criteria. Refer to Note 17 , Restructuring, for further discussion. Assets Held for Sale We classify long-lived assets or disposal groups as held for sale in the period when all of the following conditions have been met: • we have approved and committed to a plan to sell the assets or disposal group; • the asset or disposal group is available for immediate sale in its present condition; •

ssess fair value less any costs to sell at each reporting period until the asset or disposal group is no longer classified as held for sale. In the first quarter of fiscal 2026, the company announced an agreement to form a joint venture with Boyu Capital to operate Starbucks retail in China (the “disposal group”). Under the agreement, Boyu Capital will acquire up to a 60% interest in 9 Table of Contents Starbucks retail operations in China. Starbucks will retain a 40% interest in the joint venture and will continue to own and license the Starbucks brand and intellectual property to the new entity.
